questions.question: What's physics really about?
options: Complicated math that only smart people understand
Figuring out how and why things work in nature
A class in school where you memorize formulas
Only stuff that happens in laboratories
questions.explanation: Physics is just understanding how things work. It's not about being smart—it's about being curious and asking 'why?'

questions.question: Physics only matters for scientists.
questions.explanation: Physics is everywhere! Your phone, your food, your body—all physics. Everyone needs to understand it.

questions.question: Which of these is physics at work?
options: A ball falling to the ground
A bird flying because it's alive
questions.explanation: A ball falling is pure physics (gravity, motion). Growing plants involve biology, chemical reactions involve chemistry. Physics deals with motion, energy, and forces.

questions.question: If you understand why a ball falls, you can predict where it will land.
questions.explanation: Yes! Understanding the rules (physics) lets you predict what will happen. That's the whole point.

questions.question: Which order is correct for how physics works?
questions.explanation: First you see something happen (observe). Then you figure out why (understand). Then you can say what will happen next (predict). Then you use that to make cool stuff (build).
